title: Representing and Reasoning on Contextual Knowledge
abstract: One of the critical issues confronted in doing logical inference on large spatiotemporal knowledge bases is the fact that most real-world logical relationships are contextual in nature. Contextuality must be explicitly taken into account to make real-world inference tractable. This is one of the issues with which formal-logic-based AI has traditionally had the most difficulty. In this chapter we briefly explore the notion of contextual inference and the key approaches that have been taken.
